meaning: n.磨具,削具,(赌博中的)骗子
examples: The current generation of home-aloners came of age during Europe's shift from social democracy to the sharper, more individualistic climate of American-style capitalism.
The issue must be brought into sharper focus.
As children grow older, their cognitive processes become sharper.
So the market moves to new levels, both up and down, in sharper intraday moves.
Put it all together and you bring your snapshots of buying customers into sharper focus.
